old_claim.note: Public Health Inspections provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No civic-inspection dependency remains because Hippocratic individual case narratives are a clinical documentary form, not records produced by municipal sanitation offices.

source_shape.source_locator: The review describes individual Hippocratic case histories around 400 BC and their medical narrative content without linking them to civic health inspection.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The cited cases belong to the Hippocratic clinical corpus rather than Roman municipal administrative records.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The former Aedile citation concerned civic administration, while the corrected source locates case histories in clinical observation and writing; those are distinct institutional scopes.

ontology_before: Medical case notes depended on an aedile-style public health inspection office unrelated to Hippocratic clinical narratives.
ontology_after: Hippocratic case histories are modeled as written clinical records without a municipal-inspection prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: public_health_inspections is no longer a direct prerequisite for medical_case_notes.
would_reject_if: The node were respecified as municipal inspection reports rather than individual clinical case histories.
A primary source demonstrated that the cited Hippocratic cases were produced by civic sanitation inspectors.
short_reason: Clinical case histories are not municipal inspection records.
